## 1. Executive Summary

The `webbl` module is a Java-based microservice within the Mercury Services platform that handles the end-to-end lifecycle of **Web Bills of Lading (Web BL)** for the INTTRA shipping network. Its primary responsibilities are:

- **Inbound ingestion** of Bill of Lading PDF files delivered via Amazon SQS, triggered when carriers upload finalized BL documents.
- **Inbound ingestion** of ZIP-packaged BL payloads via a separate SQS queue.
- **BL resolution**: correlating PDF file names (which encode SCAC carrier code + BL number) against an Elasticsearch index and DynamoDB store to locate the matching structured BL data.
- **Outbound distribution**: fan-out of the matched BL (packaged as a ZIP containing PDF + JAXB-marshalled XML) to all eligible subscribers, either via EDI (SQS to a downstream distributor queue) or via Email (SES).
- **Archival**: serialising structured BL records (sourced from Oracle) to S3 as canonical JSON, driven by an Oracle DB poll loop.
- **Retry management**: a MySQL-backed retry table tracks PDF processing failures and re-drives them on a configurable schedule with progressive back-off.
- **REST API**: a single Dropwizard/JAX-RS endpoint for direct BL lookup by carrier and BL number.

The service is built on **Dropwizard** (JAX-RS REST framework), uses **Google Guice** for dependency injection, **MyBatis** for SQL mapping (Oracle + MySQL), **AWS SDK v2** via the internal `cloud-sdk-aws` wrapper, and **Elasticsearch 6.6.2** (via JEST client) for BL reference search.

## 3. High-Level Architecture Diagram

```
  EXTERNAL CARRIERS / MFT SYSTEM
         |            |
  [PDF files]   [ZIP files]
         |            |
         v            v
  +------+------------+------+
  |   AWS SQS Inbound Queues  |
  | inPDFQueueUrl             |
  | inZIPQueueUrl             |
  +------+------------+------+
         |            |
         v            v
  +------+------+ +---+------+
  |  SQSListener| |SQSListener|
  |  (pdf)      | | (zip)    |
  +------+------+ +---+------+
         |                |
         v                v
  +-----------+    +-------------+
  |PDF Inbound|    |ZIP Inbound  |
  |Processor  |    |Processor    |
  |Task       |    |Task         |
  |(8 threads)|    |(8 threads)  |
  +--+---+----+    +------+------+
     |   |                |
     |   | BL not found   |
     |   v                |
     | [RetryRequest]     |
     |  MySQL DB          |
     |   ^                |
     |   | (scheduled)    |
     |   |                |
  +--+---+----+           |
  |NetworkDB  |           |
  |Listener   |           |
  |(retry job)|           |
  +----+------+           |
       |                  |
       | BL found         |
       v                  v
  +----+------------------+---+
  | Elasticsearch Search       |
  | (bl type, 365-day rolling) |
  +----------------------------+
       |
       v
  +----------+
  | DynamoDB |  bill_of_lading table
  | (load    |  PK: id, SK: sequenceNumber
  | versions)|  GSI: blInttraReferenceNumber
  +----------+
       |
       v  BLVersion selected (FinalTransmission or latest)
       |
  +----+------------------+
  | OutboundService       |
  | (32 threads)          |
  +-+----+--------+-------+
    |    |        |
    |    |   Look up Subscriptions
    |    |   (Network Services cache)
    |    |
    |  [For each Subscription]
    |    |
    |    +-------> EDI Action?
    |    |           |
    |    |           v
    |    |      [Build ZIP: PDF + XML]
    |    |      [Upload to S3]
    |    |      [Send MetaData to SQS]
    |    |       distributorQueueUrl
    |    |
    |    +-------> Email Action?
    |                |
    |                v
    |           [SES Email via cloud-sdk]
    |           [SuccessTemplate / ErrorTemplate]
    |
    +-------> Error case?
                |
                v
          [Look up ERROR_EMAIL subscriptions]
          [Send error email via SES]

  +-------------------------+
  | Oracle DB Poll Loop     |   (every 30 min)
  | InttraDBListener        |
  +----+--------------------+
       |
       | SELECT WEBBL_FEED SUBMITTED
       | (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, batches of 5000)
       |
       v
  +----+---------+
  | WebBLArchive |
  | Task         |
  | (batch=50)   |
  +----+---------+
       |
       | getBlCrossReferenceType (Oracle queries)
       | map to BillOfLading canonical model
       |
       v
  [Write JSON to S3 archive bucket]
  [Update WEBBL_FEED -> COMPLETED]

  +---------------------+
  | REST API (Dropwizard)|
  | GET /{carrierId}/   |
  |     {blNumber}      |
  +---------------------+
       |
       v
  Elasticsearch search -> DynamoDB load -> BLContract JSON response

  +-----------------------------+
  | SNS Event Topic             |
  | (workflow event logging)    |
  | EventLogger -> EventPublisher|
  +-----------------------------+
```

## 4. Key Classes Reference

| Class | Package | Role / Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| `WebBLApplication` | `com.inttra.mercury.webbl` | Main entry point; builds `InttraServer`, registers all Guice modules, starts listeners |
| `WebBLConfig` | `config` | Root Dropwizard configuration POJO; holds DynamoDB, ES, SQS/SNS URLs, retry config, email config |
| `AppianWayConfig` | `config` | Nested config for SQS queue URLs, S3 workspace, DB datasources, listener/outbound feature flags |
| `RetryConfig` | `config` | Retry gap parameters: firstRetryGapInMinutes, secondRetryGapInMinutes, thirdRetryGapInMinutes, fourthRetryGapInMinutes, lastRetryGapInDays |
| `ESConfiguration` | `config` | Elasticsearch endpoint, credentials, shard/replica count, timeouts |
| `EmailSenderConfig` | `config` | SES source email address and internal email list |
| `WebBLInjector` | `config` | Primary Guice `AbstractModule`; installs JestModule, WebBLEmailModule; provides named SQSListener beans for "pdf" and "zip" |
| `InboundServiceModule` | `config` | Creates and binds PDF and ZIP `ExecutorService` thread pools (8 threads each, `SynchronousQueue`) |
| `OutboundServiceModule` | `config` | Creates and binds outbound `ExecutorService` (32 threads, `ArrayBlockingQueue(60)`) |
| `WebBLDynamoModule` | `config` | Provides `DynamoDbClientConfig` and `BLDao` (backed by `DynamoRepositoryFactory`) |
| `WebBLMessagingModule` | `config` | Provides `MessagingClient<String>` (SQS), `NotificationService` (SNS), `EventPublisher` via cloud-sdk factories |
| `WebBLStorageModule` | `config` | Provides `StorageClient` (S3) via `StorageClientFactory` |
| `WebBLLifecycleListener` | `config` | Jetty `LifeCycle.Listener`; logs executor termination status on shutdown |
| `ListenerManager` | `common.listener.support` | Dropwizard `Managed`; starts PDF/ZIP executor threads and schedules DB poll loops |
| `SQSListener` | `common.listener` | Polls SQS queue in a tight loop (long or short polling); submits batches to processor; deletes on success |
| `NetworkDBListener` | `common.listener` | Scheduled every `retryIntervalInMinutes`; loads due `RetryRequest` rows and re-drives `WebBLPDFInboundProcessorTask.reprocess()` |
| `InttraDBListener` | `common.listener` | Scheduled every 30 minutes; claims SUBMITTED rows from Oracle `WEBBL_FEED` and triggers `WebBLArchiveTask.process()` |
| `WebBLPDFInboundProcessorTask` | `inbound.pdf` | Core PDF processing: parse SQS `MetaData`, extract SCAC+BLNumber from filename, ES search, DynamoDB load, outbound dispatch; manages retry record lifecycle |
| `WebBLZipInboundProcessorTask` | `inbound.zip` | ZIP message processor; reads S3 file, delegates outbound (skeleton implementation) |
| `WebBLArchiveTask` | `inbound.xml` | Maps Oracle BL data to canonical `BillOfLading` POJO (in batches of 50) and writes JSON to S3 archive |
| `WebBLService` | `inbound.service` | Service facade over `WebBLDao`; provides claim/load/update/purge for `WebBLProcessRequest` |
| `RetryRequestService` | `inbound.service` | Service facade over `RetryRequestMapper` (MySQL); insert/update/delete/load retry records |
| `OutboundServiceImpl` | `outbound.services.impl` | Core outbound: resolves parties, fetches subscriptions, fan-out to EDI (SQS) or Email (SES); archives BL post-distribution |
| `OutboundEmailSender` | `outbound.email` | Sends success (SuccessTemplate) and error (ErrorTemplate) emails via cloud-sdk `EmailService` (SES) |
| `BLDao` | `dao` | DynamoDB repository wrapper; queries `bill_of_lading` by partition key `id` |
| `WebBLDao` | `persistence` | Oracle MyBatis DAO; assembles `BlCrossReferenceType` from multiple Oracle queries; uses `@Transactional` for claim-and-lock |
| `WebBLMapper` | `persistence` | MyBatis annotated mapper for Oracle; SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE against `WEBBL_FEED`, `BL`, `BL_COMPANY`, `BL_REFERENCE`, `BL_CONTAINER`, `BL_DOCUMENT`, `BL_COMMENT`, `SUBSCRIPTION` |
| `RetryRequestMapper` | `persistence` | MyBatis annotated mapper for MySQL `RetryRequest` table; SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE |
| `ElasticSearchService` | `elasticsearch` | JEST-based ES client; builds and executes bool queries on `bl` type; 365-day date range filter; returns best matching `BLSearchResult` |
| `BillOfLadingIndexAttributes` | `elasticsearch` | Metadata holder mapping logical attribute keys (`BillOfLadingNumber`, `BookingNumber`) to ES field names (`blReferenceNumbers`, `carrierBookingNumbers`) |
| `BLVersion` | `model` | DynamoDB entity `@DynamoDbBean`; PK=id, SK=sequenceNumber (format: `m_<millis>_<state>_<inttraRef>`); GSI on blInttraReferenceNumber; TTL=expiresOn |
| `BLContract` | `model` | Canonical in-memory BL model; stored as JSON in `BLVersion.message`; contains parties, transports, references, equipment, etc. |
| `BillOfLading` | `model` | Archive model written as JSON to S3; includes blId, blFeedId, parties, sailing, equipment, references, archiveDate |
| `RetryRequest` | `model` | MySQL row model: id, retryCount, nextRetryDate, workflowId, queueStatus (IN_QUEUE / IN_PROCESSING), workspaceFilePath |
| `BlCrossReferenceType` | `model.schema` | JAXB-annotated root XML element; marshalled to XML and included in outbound ZIP |
| `WebBLResource` | `resource` | Dropwizard JAX-RS resource; `GET /{carrierId}/{blNumber}` — performs ES search + DynamoDB load + BLVersion selection |
| `FileUtils` | `util` | Static utilities: createZipFile, getXMLFile (JAXB marshal), getBlCrossReferenceType, calcKey (S3 path), trimFileExtension |
| `SQSClient` | `common.messaging` | Thin wrapper over cloud-sdk `MessagingClient<String>`; sendMessage / receiveMessage / deleteMessage |
| `SNSClient` | `common.messaging` | Thin wrapper over cloud-sdk `NotificationService`; publish |
| `S3WorkspaceService` | `common.s3` | Wraps cloud-sdk `StorageClient`; getContent, getContentAsBytes, putObject (file or string) |
| `CacheSubscriptionService` | `networkservices.subscription` | In-memory cache over `SubscriptionService`; looks up subscriptions by INTTRA company ID or FTP ID |
| `CacheNetworkParticipantService` | `networkservices.networkparticipant` | Cache of carrier network participants; lookup by SCAC code or company ID |
| `CacheIntegrationProfileFormatService` | `networkservices.integrationprofileformat` | Cache of Integration Profile Formats; used to enrich SQS metadata for EDI actions |
| `CreateTables` | `dynamo` | Dropwizard CLI command `dynamo-create`; uses `DynamoDbAdminCommand` to create/manage DynamoDB tables |

---

## 5. PDF Inbound Processing — Detailed Data Flow

### 5.1 Normal (Attempt 0) Flow

```
  SQS InPDF Queue
       |
       | receiveMessage(maxMessages, waitTimeSeconds)
       v
  SQSListener (pdf thread)
       |
       | submit batch
       v
  WebBLPDFInboundProcessorTask.process()
  [pdfProcessor ExecutorService — 8 threads]
       |
       | for each QueueMessage<String> in batch:
       v
  execute(message, successCallback)
       |
       | Parse message.payload -> MetaData (JSON)
       |   workflowId, fileName, bucket,
       |   projections: {originalFileName, mftId, inftpfilepickuptime, ...}
       |
       | extract SCAC code from originalFileName:
       |   format: "<SCAC>_<BLNumber>.pdf"
       |   SCAC  = substring(0, indexOf('_'))
       |   BLNum = substring(indexOf('_')+1, lastIndexOf('.pdf'))
       |
       +---> SCAC or BLNum blank?
       |         |
       |         v YES
       |     errors += ValidationError(code=2000)
       |     throw BLNumberNotFound
       |         |
       |         v -> handleError (see Retry Decision Tree)
       |
       | networkParticipantService.getNetworkParticipantBySCACCode(scac)
       |
       +---> Carrier not found?
       |         |
       |         v YES
       |     errors += ValidationError(code=2002)
       |     throw CarrierNotFound
       |         |
       |         v -> handleError -> processError (send error email, no retry)
       |
       | elasticSearchService.findBillOfLadingIdByReference(
       |     Reference(BillOfLadingNumber, blNumber),
       |     networkParticipant.inttraCompanyId
       | )
       |
       +---> BL not found in ES?
       |         |
       |         v YES
       |     errors += ValidationError(code=2001)
       |     throw BillOfLadingNotFound
       |         |
       |         v -> handleError (see Retry Decision Tree)
       |
       | dao.load(searchResult.id)
       |   -> DynamoDB query PK=id, returns List<BLVersion>
       |
       | getRelevantBLVersion(blVersions)
       |   (see BLVersion Selection Algorithm, Section 9)
       |
       | outboundService.processOutbound(blVersion, metaData, errors)
       |   -> submits to outbound executor (see Section 8)
       |
       +---> retryRequest != null?
       |         YES -> retryService.delete(retryRequest) [MySQL]
       |
       | eventLogger.logCloseRunEvent(...)  [SNS publish]
       | successCallback.accept(message)    [SQS deleteMessage]
       |
       v
  WebBLResult{errors=[]}
```

### 5.2 Retry Decision Tree (handleError method)

```
  Exception caught in execute()
       |
       +---- Is BLNumberNotFound or CarrierNotFound?
       |         |
       |         v YES (non-retryable: filename/carrier issue)
       |     outboundService.processError(metaData, errors)
       |       -> send ERROR_EMAIL to error subscribers
       |     [no retry created]
       |     DONE
       |
       +---- Is BillOfLadingNotFound AND attemptCount >= MAX_RETRY_COUNT (5)?
       |         |
       |         v YES (retry exhausted)
       |     outboundService.processError(metaData, errors)
       |     retryService.delete(retryRequest)   [MySQL DELETE]
       |     DONE
       |
       +---- Is BillOfLadingNotFound AND attemptCount == 0?
       |         |
       |         v YES (first failure)
       |     Save MetaData JSON to S3:
       |       path = fileName + "/" + UUID + ".json"
       |     Create RetryRequest:
       |       queueStatus = "IN_QUEUE"
       |       workflowId  = workflowId
       |       workspaceFilePath = s3Path
       |       retryCount  = 0
       |       nextRetryDate = NOW + firstRetryGapInMinutes
       |     retryService.insert(retryRequest)   [MySQL INSERT]
       |     DONE
       |
       +---- Is BillOfLadingNotFound AND 0 < attemptCount < 5?
                 |
                 v YES (subsequent retry)
             retryRequest.retryCount += 1
             retryRequest.nextRetryDate = getNextRetryDate(...)
             retryRequest.queueStatus = "IN_QUEUE"
             retryService.update(retryRequest)   [MySQL UPDATE]
             DONE
```

---

## 6. ZIP Inbound Processing Flow

```
  SQS InZIP Queue
       |
       | receiveMessage(maxMessages, waitTimeSeconds)
       v
  SQSListener (zip thread)
       |
       | submit batch
       v
  WebBLZipInboundProcessorTask.process()
  [zipProcessor ExecutorService — 8 threads]
       |
       | for each QueueMessage<String>:
       v
  execute(message, successCallback)
       |
       | Parse message.payload -> MetaData (JSON)
       |   bucketName = metaData.getBucket()
       |   fileName   = metaData.getFileName()
       |
       | [S3 retrieval and processing — currently a skeleton]
       |   Returns WebBLResult{}
       |
       | Note: on exception, message is NOT deleted from SQS.
       |       SQS visibility timeout expires -> message becomes
       |       visible again for consumer retry.
       |
       | log: "Message not deleted. Will be retried by a consumer
       |       thread after visibility timeout."
       v
  WebBLResult{}

  NOTE: The ZIP processor is wired up and operational at the listener
  level but the S3 file retrieval and mapping logic is currently a
  placeholder (returns empty WebBLResult). Extension point for future
  ZIP-packaged BL ingest.
```

---

## 7. Oracle DB Listener Flow (InttraDBListener)

```
  ListenerManager startup
       |
       | scheduleWithFixedDelay(
       |   oracleListener::startup,
       |   initialDelay = 1 min,
       |   period       = 30 min
       | )
       v
  InttraDBListener.startup()
       |
       | Is another execution still running?
       |   YES -> skip (log "Another Job already in progress")
       |   NO  -> pollAndExecute()
       v
  pollAndExecute()
       |
       | webBLService.claimAndLoadWebBLProcessRequests()
       |   -> WebBLDao.claimAndLoadRequests()  [@Transactional]
       |       |
       |       | mapper.selectForClaimWebBLProcessRequests()
       |       |   SELECT WEBBL_FEED_ID, BL_ID
       |       |   FROM inttra.WEBBL_FEED
       |       |   WHERE PROCESSING_STATUS = 'SUBMITTED'
       |       |     AND ROWNUM <= 5000
       |       |   FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
       |       |
       |       | (for each batch of 1000 — ORA-01795 limit)
       |       | mapper.updateWebBLProcessRequests(batch, 'IN_PROCESSING')
       |       |
       |       v
       |   Returns List<WebBLProcessRequest> (up to 5000)
       |
       +---> Empty list? -> log "No Web BL records found" -> return
       |
       | executorService.submit(() -> archiveTask.process(requests))
       |   [single-thread executor, non-blocking]
       |
       | webBLService.purgeWebBLProcessRequests()
       |   DELETE FROM inttra.WEBBL_FEED
       |   WHERE PROCESSING_STATUS = 'COMPLETED'
       |     AND UPDATED_DATE < SYSDATE - 30
       v
  WebBLArchiveTask.process(requests)
       |
       | Partition into batches of 50
       |   for each batch:
       |   +-> for each WebBLProcessRequest:
       |         mapToBillOfLading(request, archived, errored, Optional.empty())
       |         |
       |         | webBLService.getBlCrossReferenceType(blId)
       |         |   -> WebBLDao.getBlCrossReferenceType(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getBLDocumentDetails(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getWebBLTransactionParties(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getBlSailing(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getLetterOfCredit(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getEquipments(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getWebBLReferences(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getBLComments(blId)
       |         |       mapper.getEmailNotifications(blId)
       |         |
       |         | MappingUtils.populate*(crossReferenceType, billOfLading, ...)
       |         |   populateControl, populateComments, populateDocument,
       |         |   populateReferences, populateParties, populateEquipments,
       |         |   populateLetterOfCredit, populateSailing
       |         |
       |         +-> success: add to archivedBillsOfLadings
       |         +-> exception: add to erroredBillsOfLadings
       |                        eventLogger.logCloseRunEvent(FAILURE)
       |
       |   writeWebBLsToS3(archivedBillsOfLadings, isPdfInbound=false)
       |       for each BillOfLading:
       |         fileName = calcKey(now, blId, workFlowId)
       |           format: "YYYY/MM/DD/HH/<blId>_<workflowId>"
       |         billOfLading.archiveDate = now (CCYYMMDDHHMMSS)
       |         s3.putObject(s3ArchiveBucket, fileName, JSON)
       |         webBLService.updateWebBLProcessRequests([bl], COMPLETED)
       |         eventLogger.logCloseRunEvent(SUCCESS)
       |
       |   if erroredBillsOfLadings not empty:
       |     webBLService.updateWebBLProcessRequests(errored, ERROR)
       v
  DONE
```

---

## 8. Outbound Distribution Flow

### 8.1 Overview

```
  OutboundServiceImpl.processOutbound(blVersion, metaData, errors)
       |
       | executorService.submit(...)
       | [outbound ExecutorService — 32 threads]
       v
  process(blVersion, metaData, errors)
       |
       | eventLogger START_WORKFLOW
       |
       | getCodedParties(blVersion)
       |   -> parse BLVersion.message as BLContract (JSON)
       |   -> collect all non-empty partyINTTRACompanyId values
       |      (parties: Shipper, Consignee, Carrier, Requestor,
       |                NotifyParty, Forwarder, ...)
       |
       +---> No coded parties? -> DONE (no subscriptions to check)
       |
       | For each coded party INTTRA company ID:
       |   cacheSubscriptionService.getSubscriptions(companyId)
       |   -> flat-map all subscription lists
       |
       | SubscriptionHelper.filterSubscriptions(blVersion, "outbound", subscriptions)
       |   -> ConditionEvaluator.evaluateConditions(
       |        conditions, AND,
       |        {contextCode_contextCode: "publishWebBL"}
       |      )
       |   -> keep only subscriptions where all conditions pass
       |
       +---> No subscriptions after filter? -> DONE
       |
       | Download PDF from S3:
       |   pdfFile = s3.getContentAsBytes(bucket, fileName) -> File
       |
       | Build JAXB XML:
       |   blCrossReferenceType = FileUtils.getBlCrossReferenceType(
       |       blVersion, metaData, networkParticipantService)
       |   xmlFile = FileUtils.getXMLFile(metaData, blCrossReferenceType)
       |     -> JAXB marshal blCrossReferenceType -> ISO-8859-1 XML
       |
       | Create ZIP:
       |   zipFile = FileUtils.createZipFile(xmlFile, pdfFile)
       |     -> pdfFile.name + ".zip"  (contains both PDF and XML)
       |
       | Upload ZIP to S3:
       |   zipFileName = originalS3Key + "/" + zipFile.getName()
       |   s3.putObject(bucket, zipFileName, zipFile)
       |
       | Add projection: fileNameToken.inputFileName = trimmedOriginalFileName
       |
       | for each Subscription:
       |   processSubscription(metaData, subscription, tokens, zipFileName, blVersion)
       |
       | archiveWebBL(blVersion, blCrossReferenceType, workflowId)
       |   (see Section 7 for archive logic)
       |
       | eventLogger CLOSE_WORKFLOW
       |
       | deleteTempFiles(pdfFile, xmlFile, zipFile)
       v
  DONE
```

### 8.2 Per-Subscription Processing

```
  processSubscription(metaData, subscription, tokens, zipFileName, blVersion)
       |
       | Create child MetaData (new workflowId, parent=original)
       |   serviceName = "webbl-outbound-subscription"
       |
       | Separate actions by ActionType:
       |   ediActions   = actions where ActionType == EDI
       |   emailActions = actions where ActionType == EMAIL
       |
       +============ EDI ACTION FLOW ==========+
       |                                        |
       | processEDI(ediActions, tokens, meta)   |
       |   for each ediAction:                  |
       |     ipf = ipfService                   |
       |           .getIntegrationProfileFormat |
       |           (action.refId)               |
       |     Set projections on MetaData:       |
       |       TARGET_ID = refId                |
       |       OUTBOUND_INTEGRATION_PROFILE     |
       |         _FORMAT_ID = refId             |
       |       OUTBOUND_FORMAT_ID = ipf.formatId|
       |       OUTBOUND_INTEGRATION_PROFILE_ID  |
       |         = ipf.integrationProfileId     |
       |       CONTEXT_CODE = "webbl"           |
       |       TARGET_TYPE = "IntegrationProfile|
       |                      Format"           |
       |     sendToSqs(metaData, sqsUrl, action)|
       |       if ActionType == SQS:            |
       |         sqsClient.sendMessage(         |
       |           action.refId, metaDataJson)  |
       |       else:                            |
       |         sqsClient.sendMessage(         |
       |           distributorQueueUrl,         |
       |           metaDataJson)                |
       |     log "Sent to distributor"          |
       +========================================+

       +============ EMAIL ACTION FLOW =========+
       |                                        |
       | processEmail(blVersion, emailActions,  |
       |              tokens, metaData)         |
       |   emails = emailActions.map(refId)     |
       |             .distinct()                |
       |   emailSender.sendSuccessEmail(        |
       |     emails, blVersion,                 |
       |     isIncludeHTMLAttachment=true,      |
       |     metaData)                          |
       |     -> parse BLContract from           |
       |        blVersion.message               |
       |     -> build vars:                     |
       |        Subject, blNumber, carrierName, |
       |        documentReceivedDate,           |
       |        sourceEmail                     |
       |     -> emailService.sendTemplateEmail( |
       |        "SuccessTemplate",              |
       |        recipients, vars)               |
       +========================================+

       | eventLogger CLOSE_WORKFLOW (subscription)
       v
  DONE

  +============ ERROR EMAIL FLOW =============+
  |                                           |
  | processErrorNotifications(metaData, errors)|
  |   errorSubscriptions =                    |
  |     cacheSubscriptionService              |
  |     .getFTPIdSubscriptions(mftId)         |
  |   filter: ActionType == ERROR_EMAIL       |
  |   collect recipient emails                |
  |   emailSender.sendErrorEmails(            |
  |     recipients, errors, metaData, false)  |
  |     -> "ErrorTemplate" email via SES      |
  +============================================+
```

---

## 9. BLVersion Selection Algorithm

The same algorithm is implemented identically in both `WebBLPDFInboundProcessorTask.getRelevantBLVersion()` and `WebBLResource.getRelevantBLVersion()`.

```
  Input: List<BLVersion> blVersions (from DynamoDB query on id)

  +---> List is null or empty?
  |         YES -> return null
  |
  +---> List has exactly 1 element?
  |         YES -> return blVersions.get(0)
  |
  +---> List has multiple elements:
            |
            | Stream all versions
            | Filter: sequenceNumber.contains("FinalTransmission")
            |
            +---> Any FinalTransmission versions found?
            |         |
            |         v YES
            |     From those, pick the one with MAX(expiresOn)
            |     return that BLVersion
            |
            +---> No FinalTransmission versions?
                      |
                      v
                  From ALL versions, pick the one with MAX(expiresOn)
                  return that BLVersion

  BLVersion.sequenceNumber format (from constructor):
    "m_<System.currentTimeMillis()>_<state>_<inttraReferenceNumber>"
    e.g.: "m_1714857600000_FinalTransmission_ITR123456"
    e.g.: "m_1714857500000_Draft_ITR123456"

  State values that can appear in sequenceNumber:
    - "FinalTransmission"  (carrier has issued final BL)
    - "Draft"              (preliminary/draft version)
    - "Replace"            (amendment/replacement)
    - other state names from BLContract.State enum
```

---

## 10. PDF Retry Schedule Design

The `RetryConfig` YAML section defines the gaps between retry attempts. The `NetworkDBListener` is scheduled at `retryIntervalInMinutes` to poll the `RetryRequest` MySQL table for records where `nextRetryDate < NOW()`.

```
  ATTEMPT 0 (initial failure — BillOfLadingNotFound)
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | Action: CREATE RetryRequest record in MySQL     |
  |   retryCount = 0                                |
  |   status     = IN_QUEUE                         |
  |   nextRetryDate = NOW() + firstRetryGapInMinutes|
  |   workspaceFilePath = s3://workspace/<path>     |
  |   (MetaData JSON saved to S3 for reprocessing)  |
  +-------------------------------------------------+
                    |
                    | (NetworkDBListener fires, nextRetryDate has passed)
                    v
  ATTEMPT 1 (retryCount becomes 1)
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | Action: UPDATE RetryRequest                     |
  |   retryCount     = 1 (was 0)                    |
  |   status         = IN_QUEUE                     |
  |   nextRetryDate += secondRetryGapInMinutes      |
  +-------------------------------------------------+
                    |
                    v
  ATTEMPT 2 (retryCount becomes 2)
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | Action: UPDATE RetryRequest                     |
  |   retryCount     = 2 (was 1)                    |
  |   status         = IN_QUEUE                     |
  |   nextRetryDate += thirdRetryGapInMinutes       |
  +-------------------------------------------------+
                    |
                    v
  ATTEMPT 3 (retryCount becomes 3)
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | Action: UPDATE RetryRequest                     |
  |   retryCount     = 3 (was 2)                    |
  |   status         = IN_QUEUE                     |
  |   nextRetryDate += fourthRetryGapInMinutes      |
  +-------------------------------------------------+
                    |
                    v
  ATTEMPT 4 (retryCount becomes 4)
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | Action: UPDATE RetryRequest                     |
  |   retryCount     = 4 (was 3)                    |
  |   status         = IN_QUEUE                     |
  |   nextRetryDate += lastRetryGapInDays (DAYS!)   |
  +-------------------------------------------------+
                    |
                    v
  ATTEMPT 5 (retryCount + 1 == 5 == MAX_RETRY_COUNT)
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | Action: EXHAUSTED                               |
  |   outboundService.processError(metaData, errors)|
  |     -> send error email to ERROR_EMAIL subs     |
  |   retryService.delete(retryRequest)             |
  |     -> DELETE FROM RetryRequest WHERE id=?      |
  +-------------------------------------------------+

  getNextRetryDate() switch on (retryRequest.retryCount + 1):
    case 2 -> nextRetryDate + secondRetryGapInMinutes (MINUTES)
    case 3 -> nextRetryDate + thirdRetryGapInMinutes  (MINUTES)
    case 4 -> nextRetryDate + fourthRetryGapInMinutes (MINUTES)
    case 5 -> nextRetryDate + lastRetryGapInDays      (DAYS)
    default-> nextRetryDate + firstRetryGapInMinutes  (MINUTES)

  During reprocess(), if an unexpected exception occurs:
    retryRequest.nextRetryDate = getNextRetryDate(request, config)
    retryRequest.retryCount   += 1
    retryRequest.queueStatus   = IN_QUEUE
    -> UPDATE RetryRequest (schedules another attempt)

  Status transitions:
    IN_QUEUE -> IN_PROCESSING (set at start of reprocess)
    IN_PROCESSING -> (implicitly consumed; deleted on success,
                      updated back to IN_QUEUE on failure)
```

---

## 11. DynamoDB Table Schema — bill_of_lading

### 11.1 Table Definition

```
  Table Name:    <prefix>bill_of_lading
  (prefix configured in BaseDynamoDbConfig.tablePrefix)

  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | Attribute              | Type     | Role  | Key  | Notes                  |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | id                     | String   | PK    | HASH | External BL identifier |
  |                        |          |       |      | (Elasticsearch siId)   |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | sequenceNumber         | String   | SK    | RANGE| Format:                |
  |                        |          |       |      | m_<millis>_<state>_    |
  |                        |          |       |      | <inttraRef>            |
  |                        |          |       |      | e.g. m_1714000000000_  |
  |                        |          |       |      | FinalTransmission_123  |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | blInttraReferenceNumber| String   | GSI PK|      | INTTRA reference #     |
  |                        |          |       |      | GSI: INTTRA_REFERENCE_ |
  |                        |          |       |      |      NUMBER_INDEX      |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | carrierId              | String   |       |      | Carrier company ID     |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | requestorId            | String   |       |      | Requestor company ID   |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | shipperId              | String   |       |      | Shipper company ID     |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | bookingNumber          | String   |       |      | Carrier booking number |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | blNumber               | String   |       |      | Bill of Lading number  |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | message                | String   |       |      | Full BLContract as JSON|
  |                        |          |       |      | (the entire BL payload)|
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+
  | expiresOn              | Number   | TTL   |      | Epoch seconds (Long)   |
  |                        |          |       |      | Converter:             |
  |                        |          |       |      | DateEpochSecondAttributeConverter |
  +------------------------+----------+-------+------+------------------------+

  DynamoDB Stream: KEYS_ONLY
    (@DynamoDBStream annotation on BLVersion)
    Only PK+SK are streamed on change events.

  Consistency: Eventually consistent reads (consistentRead=false)
```

### 11.2 Global Secondary Index (GSI)

```
  Index Name:   INTTRA_REFERENCE_NUMBER_INDEX
  Partition Key: blInttraReferenceNumber (String)
  Sort Key:      (none — lookup all versions by INTTRA reference)
  Projection:    ALL (default for Enhanced DynamoDB)

  Use case: Allows retrieval of all BLVersion records for a given
  INTTRA reference number, without knowing the primary 'id'.
```

### 11.3 sequenceNumber Format Examples

```
  m_1714857600000_FinalTransmission_ITR-987654
  m_1714856000000_Draft_
  m_1714855000000_Replace_ITR-987654

  Components:
    m_         : static prefix
    1714857600000 : System.currentTimeMillis() at record creation
    FinalTransmission : BL state (from BLContract.State enum)
    ITR-987654 : blInttraReferenceNumber (may be empty string)
```

---

## 12. Oracle Database Schema Overview

The Oracle schema is in the `inttra` schema. WebBL reads from (never writes to) the following tables:

### 12.1 Process Queue

```
  inttra.WEBBL_FEED
  +-----------------------+---------+------------------------------------------+
  | Column                | Type    | Description                              |
  +-----------------------+---------+------------------------------------------+
  | WEBBL_FEED_ID         | NUMBER  | PK; unique feed entry ID                 |
  | BL_ID                 | NUMBER  | FK to inttra.BL.BL_ID                    |
  | PROCESSING_STATUS     | VARCHAR | SUBMITTED | IN_PROCESSING | COMPLETED | ERROR |
  | CREATED_DATE          | DATE    | Record creation timestamp (for ordering) |
  | UPDATED_DATE          | DATE    | Last status update timestamp             |
  +-----------------------+---------+------------------------------------------+

  Claim query: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED (ROWNUM <= 5000)
  Purge: DELETE WHERE PROCESSING_STATUS = 'COMPLETED' AND UPDATED_DATE < SYSDATE-30
  Oracle IN clause limit: 1000 (handled by batching in WebBLDao)
```

### 12.2 Bill of Lading Core

```
  inttra.BL
  +------------------------+---------+------------------------------------------+
  | Column                 | Type    | Description                              |
  +------------------------+---------+------------------------------------------+
  | BL_ID                  | NUMBER  | PK                                       |
  | BL_NUMBER              | VARCHAR | Bill of Lading number                    |
  | BL_STATUS_ID           | NUMBER  | 1-4=DRAFT, 5-6=FINAL, 7-8=NA            |
  | BL_TYPE_ID             | NUMBER  | 1=NEGOTIABLE, 2=SEAWAY_BILL, 3=EXPRESS  |
  | CARRIER_COMPANY_ID     | NUMBER  | FK to inttra.ES_COMPANY                  |
  | REQUESTOR_COMPANY_ID   | NUMBER  | FK to inttra.ES_COMPANY                  |
  | MAIN_VESSEL            | VARCHAR | Vessel name                              |
  | MAIN_VOYAGE            | VARCHAR | Voyage number                            |
  | SAIL_DATE              | DATE    | Sailing date                             |
  | LOAD_PORT              | VARCHAR | Port of loading                          |
  | DISCHARGE_PORT         | VARCHAR | Port of discharge                        |
  | PLACE_OF_RECEIPT       | VARCHAR | Place of receipt                         |
  | PLACE_OF_DELIVERY      | VARCHAR | Place of delivery                        |
  | ISSUING_OFFICE         | VARCHAR | Issuing office                           |
  +------------------------+---------+------------------------------------------+
```

### 12.3 BL Parties

```
  inttra.BL_COMPANY        (linked via BL_ACTIVITY_TYPE for role lookup)
  inttra.BL_ACTIVITY_TYPE  (ACTIVITY_TYPE_ID, DESCRIPTION e.g. "Notify Party")
  inttra.ES_COMPANY        (COMPANY_ID, COMPANY_NAME — for Carrier and Requestor)

  Role mapping (from getWebBLTransactionParties query):
    BL_ACTIVITY_TYPE.DESCRIPTION == "Notify Party" -> "BLNotifyParty"
    Otherwise                                       -> DESCRIPTION as-is
    Special UNION arms:
      "Carrier"   from ES_COMPANY via b.CARRIER_COMPANY_ID
      "Requestor" from ES_COMPANY via b.REQUESTOR_COMPANY_ID
```

### 12.4 BL References

```
  inttra.BL_REFERENCE
  +----------------------+--------+-------------------------------------------+
  | Column               | Type   | Description                               |
  +----------------------+--------+-------------------------------------------+
  | BL_ID                | NUMBER | FK to inttra.BL                           |
  | BL_REFERENCE_TYPE_ID | NUMBER | 1=BookingNumber, 2=ShipperRef,            |
  |                      |        | 3=FreightForwarderRef, 4=INTTRASINumber,  |
  |                      |        | else=OtherReferenceNumber                 |
  | REFERENCE_NUMBER     | VARCHAR| Reference value                           |
  +----------------------+--------+-------------------------------------------+
```

### 12.5 BL Document Details

```
  inttra.BL_DOCUMENT
  +---------------------------+--------+---------------------------------------+
  | Column                    | Type   | Description                           |
  +---------------------------+--------+---------------------------------------+
  | BL_DOCUMENT_ID            | NUMBER | PK                                    |
  | BL_ID                     | NUMBER | FK to inttra.BL                       |
  | CARRIER_SEQUENCE_NUMBER   | VARCHAR| BL version sequence number            |
  | BL_RATED_STATUS_ID        | NUMBER | 1=U, 2=Y, 3=N                         |
  | BL_COPY_STATUS_ID         | NUMBER | 1=U, 2=N, 3=Y                         |
  | STOCK_REQUIRED            | NUMBER | 1=Y, else=N                           |
  | DOCUMENT_CREATION_DATE    | DATE   | Document creation date                |
  | URL                       | VARCHAR| Document URL                          |
  +---------------------------+--------+---------------------------------------+
  Query: latest BL_DOCUMENT_ID for given BL_ID (MAX subquery)
```

### 12.6 Equipment

```
  inttra.BL_CONTAINER
  +------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------+
  | Column           | Type   | Description                                   |
  +------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------+
  | BL_ID            | NUMBER | FK to inttra.BL                               |
  | CONTAINER_NUMBER | VARCHAR| Equipment/container number                    |
  | CONTAINER_TYPE   | VARCHAR| Equipment type code                           |
  +------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------+
```

### 12.7 Other BL Tables

```
  inttra.BL_LETTER_OF_CREDIT  (VALUE, ISSUE_DATE, EXPIRATION_DATE)
  inttra.BL_COMMENT            (latest BL_COMMENT_ID, bl_comment text)
  inttra.SUBSCRIPTION          (email notifications; joined via SUBSCRIPTION_FORMAT,
                                SUBSCRIPTION_DEFINITION)
```

---

## 13. MySQL Schema — RetryRequest Table

```
  Database: Network DB (AppianWayConfig.networkDataBase)
  Table:    RetryRequest

  +-------------------+--------------+------+-----+----------------------------+
  | Column            | Type         | Null | Key | Description                |
  +-------------------+--------------+------+-----+----------------------------+
  | id                | VARCHAR / INT| NO   | PK  | Auto-generated row ID      |
  | workspaceFilePath | VARCHAR      | YES  |     | S3 path to MetaData JSON   |
  |                   |              |      |     | (for replay on retry)      |
  | retryCount        | INT          | YES  |     | Number of attempts so far  |
  |                   |              |      |     | (0 = first failure stored) |
  | nextRetryDate     | DATETIME     | YES  |     | When to attempt next retry |
  | workflowId        | VARCHAR      | YES  |     | Original SQS workflow ID   |
  | status            | VARCHAR      | YES  |     | IN_QUEUE / IN_PROCESSING   |
  +-------------------+--------------+------+-----+----------------------------+

  Indexes implied by query patterns:
    - Primary key on id
    - Recommend index on (status, nextRetryDate) for the poll query:
        SELECT ... WHERE nextRetryDate < SYSDATE() AND status = 'IN_QUEUE'

  Note: RetryRequestMapper uses MySQL syntax (SYSDATE(), MySQL FROM Dual).
  The table resides in the Network MySQL database, while WEBBL_FEED
  resides in the Oracle inttra schema.

  Lifecycle:
    INSERT  -> on first BillOfLadingNotFound (attempt 0)
    UPDATE  -> on subsequent failures (attempts 1-4)
    DELETE  -> on success (BL found and processed) or exhaustion (attempt >= 5)
```

---

## 14. Elasticsearch Index Design

WebBL uses Elasticsearch 6.6.2 via the **JEST** HTTP client.

### 14.1 Index Structure

```
  Index alias: _all   (searches across all indices)
  Type:        bl

  The 'bl' type corresponds to ElasticsearchTypeName.bl and
  BillOfLadingIndexAttributes.getBillOfLadingAttributes().

  BLSearchResult fields returned per hit:
  +----------------------------+--------+--------------------------------------+
  | ES Field Name              | Java   | Description                          |
  +----------------------------+--------+--------------------------------------+
  | siId                       | String | DynamoDB partition key (id field)    |
  |                            |        | = BLVersion.id                       |
  | carrierId                  | Integer| Carrier INTTRA company ID            |
  | companiesWithAccess        | List   | All company IDs with BL access       |
  | blReferenceNumbers         | List   | BL reference numbers                 |
  | carrierBookingNumbers      | List   | Carrier booking numbers              |
  | lastModifiedDateUtc        | String | Last modification timestamp (UTC)    |
  +----------------------------+--------+--------------------------------------+
```

### 14.2 Search Query Construction

```
  Query Type: BoolQuery (Elasticsearch JSON DSL)

  Must clauses:
    1. _type == "bl"                        (index type filter)
    2. lastModifiedDateUtc >= "now-365d/d"  (365-day rolling window)
       lastModifiedDateUtc <= "now/d"
    3. carrierId == <inttraCompanyId>       (carrier filter)

  Should clauses (minimumShouldMatch=1):
    For ReferenceType.BillOfLadingNumber:
      matchPhrase(blReferenceNumbers, <blNumber>)
    For ReferenceType.BookingNumber:
      matchPhrase(carrierBookingNumbers, <bookingNumber>)

  Size: 10000 results maximum
  Scroll: 1 minute timeout (for large result sets)
  Source includes: all attribute names from BillOfLadingIndexAttributes

  Result selection: max(lastModifiedDateUtc) from all hits
    -> returns Optional<BLSearchResult>
```

### 14.3 ESConfiguration Properties

```
  numberOfShards:     3
  numberOfReplicas:   1
  connTimeoutMillis:  3000 ms
  readTimeoutMillis:  3000 ms
  maxRetries:         3
  indexingEnabled:    false  (WebBL is read-only against ES)
```

---

## 15. Thread Pool Topology

```
  +=====================================================================+
  |                    WEBBL THREAD POOL TOPOLOGY                       |
  +=====================================================================+

  LISTENER THREADS (ListenerManager — 4 dedicated threads)
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+
  | pdfExecutor    : SingleThreadExecutor                           |
  |   -> SQSListener(pdf).startup()  [infinite poll loop]          |
  |                                                                 |
  | zipExecutor    : SingleThreadExecutor                           |
  |   -> SQSListener(zip).startup()  [infinite poll loop]          |
  |                                                                 |
  | scheduledJobExecutor : SingleThreadScheduledExecutor            |
  |   -> NetworkDBListener.startup()                                |
  |      period: retryIntervalInMinutes                             |
  |      [polls MySQL RetryRequest, re-drives PDF processor]        |
  |                                                                 |
  | oracleScheduledJobExecutor : SingleThreadScheduledExecutor      |
  |   -> InttraDBListener.startup()                                 |
  |      period: 30 minutes                                         |
  |      [polls Oracle WEBBL_FEED, archives to S3]                  |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+

  INBOUND PROCESSOR POOLS (InboundServiceModule — 16 threads total)
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+
  | pdfProcessor : ThreadPoolExecutor                               |
  |   minThreads: 8    maxThreads: 8                               |
  |   workQueue:  SynchronousQueue (no buffering)                  |
  |   keepAlive:  0s                                               |
  |   onReject:   CallerRunsPolicy                                 |
  |   shutdown:   30s                                              |
  |   -> WebBLPDFInboundProcessorTask.process()                    |
  |                                                                 |
  | zipProcessor : ThreadPoolExecutor                               |
  |   minThreads: 8    maxThreads: 8                               |
  |   workQueue:  SynchronousQueue (no buffering)                  |
  |   keepAlive:  0s                                               |
  |   onReject:   CallerRunsPolicy                                 |
  |   shutdown:   30s                                              |
  |   -> WebBLZipInboundProcessorTask.process()                    |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+

  OUTBOUND PROCESSOR POOL (OutboundServiceModule — 32 threads)
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+
  | outbound : ThreadPoolExecutor                                   |
  |   minThreads: 32   maxThreads: 32                              |
  |   workQueue:  ArrayBlockingQueue(60)                           |
  |               (comment: 32 threads * ~10s/record = 96 max      |
  |                throughput per 30s; 60 chosen as safe buffer)   |
  |   keepAlive:  0s                                               |
  |   onReject:   CallerRunsPolicy                                 |
  |   shutdown:   30s                                              |
  |   -> OutboundServiceImpl.process()                             |
  |   -> OutboundServiceImpl.processErrorNotifications()           |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+

  ORACLE ARCHIVE WORKER (InttraDBListener — 1 thread)
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+
  | InttraDBListener.executorService : SingleThreadExecutor         |
  |   -> WebBLArchiveTask.process() [runs asynchronously]          |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+

  NETWORK DB RETRY WORKER (NetworkDBListener — 1 thread)
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+
  | NetworkDBListener.executorService : SingleThreadExecutor        |
  |   -> WebBLPDFInboundProcessorTask.reprocess() [runs async]     |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------------+

  QUEUE TYPE CHOICE RATIONALE:
    SynchronousQueue (PDF/ZIP):
      Zero-capacity handoff queue. If all 8 threads are busy, the
      submitting thread (SQSListener) blocks until a worker is free,
      creating natural back-pressure that throttles SQS polling.
      This prevents unbounded in-memory accumulation of SQS messages.

    ArrayBlockingQueue(60) (Outbound):
      Bounded buffer allows outbound tasks to be queued while workers
      process earlier submissions. Capacity chosen so shutdown
      within 30s is feasible (60 tasks / 32 workers * ~10s = ~19s).
      CallerRunsPolicy means the submitting inbound thread processes
      the task directly when the queue is full, naturally throttling
      the inbound path.

  TOTAL MANAGED THREADS:
    Listener threads:            4  (2 single + 2 scheduled)
    PDF processor:               8
    ZIP processor:               8
    Outbound:                   32
    Oracle archive worker:       1
    Network DB retry worker:     1
    Dropwizard Jetty HTTP:     managed by Dropwizard
    -------------------------------------------
    Application thread total:   54  (excluding HTTP)
```

---

## 16. Guice Dependency Injection Module Wiring

```
  InttraServer<WebBLConfig>.builder()
       |
       +-> WebBLInjector(config, env)                   [1st module]
       |     install(JestModule(esConfig))
       |       -> binds JestClient (Elasticsearch)
       |     install(WebBLEmailModule(config))
       |       -> binds EmailService (SES cloud-sdk)
       |       -> binds @Named("sourceEmail") String
       |     bind(AuthClient)      .asEagerSingleton()
       |     bind(Clock)           .toInstance(Clock.systemUTC())
       |     bind ServiceDefinition named beans
       |     @Provides @Named("zip")  SQSListener
       |     @Provides @Named("pdf")  SQSListener
       |
       +-> WebBLStorageModule(config)                    [2nd module]
       |     @Provides StorageClient (S3 via cloud-sdk)
       |
       +-> WebBLMessagingModule(config)                  [3rd module]
       |     @Provides MessagingClient<String> (SQS via cloud-sdk)
       |     @Provides NotificationService     (SNS via cloud-sdk)
       |     @Provides EventPublisher          (SNS publish lambda)
       |
       +-> InboundServiceModule(env)                     [4th module]
       |     bind @Named("pdfProcessor") ExecutorService -> TPE(8)
       |     bind @Named("pdfProcessor") Queue           -> SynchronousQueue
       |     bind @Named("zipProcessor") ExecutorService -> TPE(8)
       |     bind @Named("zipProcessor") Queue           -> SynchronousQueue
       |
       +-> OutboundServiceModule(appianWayConfig, env)   [5th module]
       |     bind @Named("outbound") Queue    -> ArrayBlockingQueue(60)
       |     bind @Named("outbound") ExecutorService -> TPE(32)
       |     bind OutboundService   -> OutboundServiceImpl
       |
       +-> WebBLDynamoModule(config, env)                [6th module]
       |     @Provides DynamoDbClientConfig
       |     @Provides BLDao (wraps DynamoRepositoryFactory)
       |
       +-> MyCustomBatisModule (MySQL — network DB)      [7th module]
       |     name:    "mySql-webbl"
       |     mappers: [RetryRequestMapper]
       |     -> binds RetryRequestMapper (MyBatis)
       |
       +-> MyCustomBatisModule (Oracle — inttra DB)      [8th module]
             name:           "oracle-webbl"
             mappers:        [WebBLMapper]
             transactional:  [WebBLDao]
             -> binds WebBLMapper (MyBatis)
             -> enables @Transactional on WebBLDao

  Key singleton bindings resolved by Guice:
    BLDao                          <- WebBLDynamoModule
    WebBLDao                       <- MyCustomBatisModule Oracle
    WebBLMapper                    <- MyCustomBatisModule Oracle
    RetryRequestMapper             <- MyCustomBatisModule MySQL
    ElasticSearchService           <- JestModule (JestClient injected)
    SQSClient                      <- WebBLInjector (MessagingClient injected)
    S3WorkspaceService             <- WebBLStorageModule (StorageClient injected)
    OutboundEmailSender            <- WebBLEmailModule
    CacheSubscriptionService       <- NetworkServiceClient (from commons)
    CacheNetworkParticipantService <- NetworkServiceClient (from commons)
    CacheIntegrationProfileFormatService <- NetworkServiceClient (from commons)
    WebBLPDFInboundProcessorTask   <- Guice @Singleton
    WebBLZipInboundProcessorTask   <- Guice @Singleton
    WebBLArchiveTask               <- Guice injected
    WebBLResource                  <- Dropwizard resource registration
    EventLogger                    <- commons (backed by EventPublisher)
```

---

## 17. REST API Summary

Base path: `/`  
Framework: Dropwizard JAX-RS (Jakarta EE)  
Auth: `InttraPrincipal` from SecurityContext  
Formats: `application/json` (consume + produce)  
Swagger: Generated to `generated/openapi-webbl.json` and `.yaml`

| Method | Path | Auth Required | Description | Response Codes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/{carrierId}/{blNumber}` | Yes (companyId must == 1000) | Retrieves the best matching Bill of Lading contract for the given carrier ID and BL number. Performs ES search then DynamoDB load then BLVersion selection. Returns `BLContract` JSON. | 200 OK (BLContract), 403 Forbidden (wrong company), 404 Not Found |

### 17.1 GET /{carrierId}/{blNumber} Detail

```
  Request:
    Path params:
      carrierId : Integer  — INTTRA company ID of the carrier
      blNumber  : String   — Bill of Lading reference number
    Header:
      Authorization: Bearer <token>

  Processing:
    1. Auth check: currentUser.companyId != 1000 -> 403 Forbidden
    2. ES search: findBillOfLadingIdByReference(
         Reference(BillOfLadingNumber, blNumber), carrierId)
    3. If no ES result -> 404 Not Found
    4. dao.load(searchResult.id) -> List<BLVersion> from DynamoDB
    5. getRelevantBLVersion(blVersions) -> select best version
    6. If null -> 404 Not Found
    7. Parse blVersion.message as BLContract (JSON)
    8. Return Response.ok(blContract) -> 200

  Response body (200): BLContract JSON
    {
      "state": "FinalTransmission|Draft|Replace",
      "documentType": "BillOfLading|SeaWayBill|MemoBill",
      "parties": [...],
      "transports": [...],
      "references": [...],
      "equipments": [...],
      "creationDate": {...},
      "generalComments": [...],
      ...
    }
```

---

## 18. Configuration Properties Reference

The following properties are defined in `WebBLConfig` and nested configuration classes. They map to the Dropwizard `config.yaml` file.

### 18.1 Top-level WebBLConfig

| YAML Key | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dynamoDbConfig` | `BaseDynamoDbConfig` | Yes | DynamoDB connection (endpoint, region, tablePrefix, etc.) |
| `appianWayConfig` | `AppianWayConfig` | Yes | All inbound/outbound queue URLs, DB datasources, feature flags |
| `blElasticSearchConfig` | `ESConfiguration` | Yes | Elasticsearch endpoint and credentials |
| `snsEventTopicArn` | String | Yes | SNS topic ARN for workflow event publishing |
| `retryConfig` | `RetryConfig` | Yes | Retry gap configuration |
| `s3ArchiveBucket` | String | Yes | S3 bucket name for BL JSON archive |
| `emailSenderConfig` | `EmailSenderConfig` | Yes | SES source email and internal email list |

### 18.2 AppianWayConfig

| YAML Key | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `s3WorkSpaceLocation` | String | (required) | S3 bucket for PDF MetaData JSON workspace |
| `transformerSQSUrl` | String | (required) | Transformer SQS queue URL (upstream) |
| `inPDFQueueUrl` | String | (required) | SQS URL for inbound PDF messages |
| `inZIPQueueUrl` | String | (required) | SQS URL for inbound ZIP messages |
| `distributorQueueUrl` | String | (required) | SQS URL for outbound EDI distribution |
| `snsTopicARN` | String | (required) | SNS topic ARN (legacy, also used for notifications) |
| `waitTimeSeconds` | int | 0 | SQS long-polling wait time (0=short poll, 1-20=long poll) |
| `maxNumberOfMessages` | int | (required) | Max SQS messages per receive call (max 10) |
| `parallelism` | int | (required) | Parallelism hint (used by listener configuration) |
| `listenerEnabled` | boolean | true | Feature flag: start SQS and DB listeners on startup |
| `outboundEnabled` | boolean | true | Feature flag: create outbound thread pool and bind OutboundServiceImpl |
| `networkDataBase` | `DataSourceFactory` | (required) | MySQL (Network DB) datasource for RetryRequest table |
| `inttraDatabase` | `DataSourceFactory` | (required) | Oracle (INTTRA DB) datasource for WebBL_FEED, BL tables |

### 18.3 RetryConfig

| YAML Key | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `retryIntervalInMinutes` | Integer | How often NetworkDBListener fires (scheduled poll interval) |
| `firstRetryGapInMinutes` | Integer | Gap from initial failure to first retry attempt |
| `secondRetryGapInMinutes` | Integer | Gap added to nextRetryDate for 2nd attempt |
| `thirdRetryGapInMinutes` | Integer | Gap added to nextRetryDate for 3rd attempt |
| `fourthRetryGapInMinutes` | Integer | Gap added to nextRetryDate for 4th attempt |
| `lastRetryGapInDays` | Integer | Gap in DAYS added to nextRetryDate for 5th (final) attempt |

### 18.4 ESConfiguration

| YAML Key | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `endpointUrl` | String | (required) | Elasticsearch endpoint URL |
| `region` | String | — | AWS region (for signed ES requests) |
| `service` | String | — | AWS service name for signing |
| `username` | String | — | Basic auth username |
| `password` | String | — | Basic auth password |
| `numberOfShards` | Integer | 3 | Number of shards (for index creation, not used by WebBL read-only) |
| `numberOfReplicas` | Integer | 1 | Number of replicas |
| `indexingEnabled` | boolean | false | WebBL does not write to ES (read-only consumer) |
| `connTimeoutMillis` | int | 3000 | Connection timeout in milliseconds |
| `readTimeoutMillis` | int | 3000 | Read timeout in milliseconds |
| `maxRetries` | int | 3 | Maximum retry attempts for ES requests |

### 18.5 EmailSenderConfig

| YAML Key | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sourceEmail` | String | SES "From" address for success and error emails |
| `internalEmails` | List\<String\> | Internal team email addresses (BCC/CC usage) |

### 18.6 Feature Flags Summary

| Flag | Location | Effect when false |
|---|---|---|
| `listenerEnabled` | AppianWayConfig | SQS listeners and DB poll loops are NOT started. Service only serves REST API. |
| `outboundEnabled` | AppianWayConfig | Outbound thread pool is NOT created. `OutboundService` is NOT bound. No outbound distribution. |

---

*End of WebBL Architecture and Design Document*
